name: Java Collections Framework Hierarchy
description: The Java Collections Framework (JCF) is a unified architecture for representing and manipulating collections. This visualization outlines the hierarchy of interfaces and their concrete implementations. The framework is divided into two primary branches: the Collection hierarchy (dealing with groups of objects) and the Map hierarchy (dealing with key-value pairs). Proper selection of a collection type is vital for optimizing time and space complexity in Java applications.
code:
graph TD
  subgraph RootInter [Root Interfaces]
    IT[Iterable] --> CO[Collection]
  end

  subgraph ListHierarchy [List - Ordered Collections]
    CO --> LI[List]
    LI --> AL[ArrayList]
    LI --> LL[LinkedList]
    LI --> VC[Vector]
    VC --> ST[Stack]
  end

  subgraph SetHierarchy [Set - Unique Elements]
    CO --> SE[Set]
    SE --> HS[HashSet]
    SE --> LHS[LinkedHashSet]
    SE --> SS[SortedSet]
    SS --> TS[TreeSet]
  end

  subgraph QueueHierarchy [Queue/Deque - Processing Order]
    CO --> QU[Queue]
    QU --> PQ[PriorityQueue]
    QU --> DQ[Deque]
    DQ --> AD[ArrayDeque]
    DQ --> LL
  end

  subgraph MapHierarchy [Map - Key/Value Pairs]
    MA[Map] --> HM[HashMap]
    MA --> LHM[LinkedHashMap]
    MA --> HT[Hashtable]
    MA --> SM[SortedMap]
    SM --> TM[TreeMap]
  end

  %% Styling classes for visibility
  classDef interface fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b,stroke-width:2px,color:#01579b,font-weight:bold
  classDef concrete fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#fbc02d,stroke-width:2px,color:#424242
  
  %% Applying classes
  class IT,CO,LI,SE,SS,QU,DQ,MA,SM interface
  class AL,LL,VC,ST,HS,LHS,TS,PQ,AD,HM,LHM,HT,TM concrete

  %% Subgraph Styling
  style RootInter fill:none,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style ListHierarchy fill:none,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style SetHierarchy fill:none,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style QueueHierarchy fill:none,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style MapHierarchy fill:none,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
